mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending demand contraction, causing equity investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment trigger, or the predictive Rank IC drops below zero over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and housing starts indicates broad-based economic expansion that boosts corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon when both series rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 50 basis points in 20-day forward returns over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO rolling 3-month changes versus 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances indicate increasing household leverage and spending capacity, which translates directly into higher retail and service sector revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The forward return differential between high-growth and low-growth RSAFS periods is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, the cost of capital becomes restrictive enough to compress equity multiples and trigger a rotation out of growth stocks, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.5 over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment grows while the unemployment rate stagnates or rises indicates structural labor market weakness and potential overhiring, signaling impending earnings revisions downward and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE level fails to explain more than 1% of the variance in 20-day forward SPX returns in a multivariate regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production relative to historical trends signals supply chain bottlenecks resolving or demand collapsing, which historically precedes equity drawdowns and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive SPX forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the cumulative return of a long-only strategy based on this trigger underperforms the buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 10% over a 5-year test.
